notes: Tested whether the engineering layer contradicts, weakens, or inverts the traditional meaning. (1) Does it contradict the imam-scoped specificity? No — the three-condition narrowing preserves it directly. (2) Does it weaken pāpam? No — translated as 'organisational debt' which preserves the causal-residue meaning rather than collapsing to moralism. (3) Does it invert the kīrti-loss? No — preserved as 'technical reputation that depended on shipping under pressure.' (4) Does it import the 'never-quit' connotation Sanskrit lacks? Explicitly no — the falsifiability and counter_example carve out legitimate exits. Reverse-translate: a Sanskrit reader reading only the engineering layer would reconstruct a stance compatible with imam dharmyam saṅgrāmam + sva-dharmaṁ kīrtiṁ ca hitvā + pāpam — the imam-specificity, the loss-pair, and the causal-residue are all reconstructable. PASS on both 3.6 and 7.10.
